qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Crafted Action Text HTML content attachment crashes the render pipeline
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an Action Text and Action View render-time exception path, while GHSA-9xrj-h377-fr87 is a path traversal in Active Storage `DiskService#path_for` that lets blob keys escape the disk storage root.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory root cause in Active Storage `DiskService#path_for`; the accepted session finding is a separate Action Text render-time denial-of-service bug.
evidence: The advisory and Rails fix are centered on `activestorage/lib/active_storage/service/disk_service.rb`, where `DiskService#path_for` failed to keep resolved blob-key paths inside the storage root.
The only final qualifying finding in `.judge/input_manifest.json` is `actiontext_content_attachment_render_dos`.

That finding's reported code locations are in `actiontext/...` and `actionview/...`, and its exploit path is a crafted `<action-text-attachment>` render crash ending in `ActionView::Template::Error` from `ActionView::Renderer::AbstractRenderer#merge_prefix_into_object_path`.
The finding does not identify `ActiveStorage::Service::DiskService`, blob-key path resolution, or arbitrary file read/write/delete via path traversal.
